families.title: Management
families.summary: Manage sandbox records, compact layer stacks, and export published changes.
families.description: Create, destroy, list, and inspect sandbox records; compact published layer stacks; and export published changes. Daemons are managed as part of sandbox lifecycle behavior, not as standalone manager operations.

operations.name: create_sandbox
operations.summary: Create a host-side sandbox record and runtime sandbox.
operations.description: Create a host-side sandbox record, create the runtime sandbox, and start its daemon.

args.name: image
args.help: Container image used to create the sandbox.

args.name: workspace_root
args.help: Absolute host workspace directory bind-mounted into this sandbox.

args.name: count
args.help: Number of sandboxes to create (minimum 1). Values greater than 1 use a shared read-only workspace base.

operations.name: destroy_sandbox
operations.summary: Destroy a host-side sandbox and remove it from the registry.
operations.description: Stop the sandbox daemon, destroy the runtime sandbox, and remove the host-side sandbox record.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Sandbox id.

operations.name: list_sandboxes
operations.summary: List sandbox records known to the manager.
operations.description: List sandbox records known to the manager, including lifecycle state and configured daemon endpoint metadata.

operations.name: inspect_sandbox
operations.description: Inspect one sandbox record, including lifecycle state, workspace root, and configured daemon endpoint metadata.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Sandbox id.

operations.name: squash_layerstacks
operations.summary: Squash a sandbox's layer stack and live-remount its sessions.
operations.description: Squash every squashable block of the selected sandbox's published layers into equivalent flattened layers and migrate live workspace sessions onto the compact chains. Forwards one squash_layerstack request to the sandbox daemon.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Sandbox id.

operations.name: export_changes
operations.summary: Export a sandbox's published changes to a host path.
operations.description: Fold every published layer above the base (newest-wins, whiteout/opaque aware) into a compressed delta stream, fetch it from the sandbox daemon, and apply it onto --dest or write it as an archive. Forwards export_layerstack and read_export_chunk requests to the sandbox daemon.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Sandbox id.

args.name: dest
args.help: Absolute host destination: directory for dir format, archive file for tar formats.

args.name: format
args.help: Output format: dir, tar, or tar-zst.

families.title: Command
families.summary: Run, interact with, and inspect commands.
families.description: Run, interact with, and inspect commands inside the active sandbox runtime.

families.title: File
families.summary: Read, write, edit, and inspect workspace files.
families.description: Read, write, and edit files against the layerstack snapshot or a live workspace session, and query per-line ownership over the publish auditability log.

operations.name: exec_command
operations.summary: Start a command in a workspace session.
operations.description: Start a shell command in a workspace session. With workspace_session_id, run inside that existing session. Without it, exec_command creates an automatic session with finalize policy publish_then_destroy: after its last command reaches terminal state, the runtime captures and publishes the session's changes to the layerstack, then destroys the session. Explicitly managed sessions remain alive until internal teardown and discard unpublished changes when torn down. File operations and remounts run under the session's admission gate and neither extend nor trigger the session lifecycle. If the command is still running after the initial wait, the response includes a command_session_id usable with read_command_lines or write_command_stdin; a still-running command stays terminable through write_command_stdin (Ctrl-C or Ctrl-D).

args.name: workspace_session_id
args.help: Existing workspace session id to run inside. Omit to create a session with finalize policy publish_then_destroy.

args.name: cmd
args.help: Shell command text.

args.name: timeout_ms
args.help: Command timeout in milliseconds.

args.name: yield_time_ms
args.help: Initial output wait in milliseconds.

cli.usage: sandbox-runtime-cli --sandbox-id ID exec_command [--workspace-session-id ID] [--timeout-ms N] [--yield-time-ms N] COMMAND

operations.name: write_command_stdin
operations.summary: Write text to a running command stdin.
operations.description: Append text to the stdin stream of a running command session and return a bounded output yield.

args.name: command_session_id
args.help: Command session id returned by exec_command.

args.name: stdin
args.help: Text to write to stdin.

args.name: yield_time_ms
args.help: Output wait after writing stdin.

cli.usage: sandbox-runtime-cli --sandbox-id ID write_command_stdin --command-session-id ID [--yield-time-ms N] TEXT

operations.name: read_command_lines
operations.summary: Read command output by line offset.
operations.description: Read rendered command output for a command session using stable line offsets.

args.name: command_session_id
args.help: Command session id returned by exec_command.

args.name: start_offset
args.help: First transcript line offset. Defaults to 0.

args.name: limit
args.help: Maximum transcript rows to return. Defaults to 200; maximum 1000.

cli.usage: sandbox-runtime-cli --sandbox-id ID read_command_lines --command-session-id ID [--start-offset N] [--limit N]

operations.name: file_read
operations.summary: Read a text file from the snapshot or a session.
operations.description: Read a UTF-8 text window from a repository-relative or workspace-root-absolute path. With workspace_session_id the read runs inside that live session's mounted workspace; without it the read projects the latest published snapshot.

args.name: path
args.help: Repository-relative or workspace-root-absolute path to read.

args.name: offset
args.help: 1-indexed line number to start reading from. Defaults to 1.

args.name: limit
args.help: Maximum number of lines to read. Defaults to 2000; must be 1..=2000.

args.name: workspace_session_id
args.help: Existing workspace session id to read inside. Omit to read the snapshot.

cli.usage: sandbox-runtime-cli --sandbox-id ID file_read --path FILE [--offset N] [--limit N] [--workspace-session-id ID]

operations.name: file_write
operations.summary: Overwrite a file in the snapshot or a session.
operations.description: Write content to a repository-relative or workspace-root-absolute path. With workspace_session_id the write lands in that live session's mounted workspace and is attributed on capture; without it the write publishes one layer attributed to operation:<request_id>.

args.name: path
args.help: Repository-relative or workspace-root-absolute path to write.

args.name: content
args.help: File content to write.

args.name: workspace_session_id
args.help: Existing workspace session id to write inside. Omit to publish a layer.

cli.usage: sandbox-runtime-cli --sandbox-id ID file_write --path FILE --content TEXT [--workspace-session-id ID]

operations.name: file_edit
operations.summary: Apply ordered string edits to a file.
operations.description: Apply an ordered list of exact-string replacements to a repository-relative or workspace-root-absolute path. Each old_string must be found and unique unless replace_all is set. With workspace_session_id the edit runs inside that live session; without it the edit publishes one layer attributed to operation:<request_id>.

args.name: path
args.help: Repository-relative or workspace-root-absolute path to edit.

args.name: edits
args.help: JSON array of { old_string, new_string, replace_all? } edits, applied in order.

args.name: workspace_session_id
args.help: Existing workspace session id to edit inside. Omit to publish a layer.

cli.usage: sandbox-runtime-cli --sandbox-id ID file_edit --path FILE --edits JSON [--workspace-session-id ID]

operations.name: file_blame
operations.summary: Show per-line ownership for a published file.
operations.description: Return each line's owner for a published path, tiling the whole file from the latest auditability event. The owner is an opaque string (workspace_session:<id> | operation:<id> | original | unknown).

args.name: path
args.help: Repository-relative path to blame.

families.title: Observability
families.summary: Inspect traces, events, and resource stats for a sandbox.
families.description: Read a sandbox's observability stream — span waterfalls, domain events, cgroup/disk resource series, and live state. Snapshot can also aggregate ready manager-known sandboxes when --sandbox-id is omitted.

operations.name: snapshot
operations.description: Show current state from the runtime registry for one sandbox, or aggregate ready manager-known sandboxes when --sandbox-id is omitted: sandbox lifecycle state, workspaces (with layer counts), in-flight executions, and the latest resource sample per scope. Served live; does not read the log.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Optional target sandbox id. When omitted, the manager queries all ready sandboxes.

operations.name: trace
operations.summary: Render one flow as a span waterfall.
operations.description: Fold the log into a span waterfall for one trace: spans nested by parent, offset by start, with attached events inline. Use --trace-id last for the most recent root trace.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

args.name: trace_id
args.help: Trace id to render, or 'last' for the most recent root trace.

operations.name: events
operations.description: Fold the log into a flat, cross-trace stream of point-in-time events (lease, errors, …), newest first. Filter by exact name and/or a start timestamp, and cap to the newest N with --last-n.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

args.name: name
args.help: Filter to events with this exact name (e.g. lease.acquired).

args.name: since_ms
args.help: Only events at or after this unix-ms timestamp.

args.name: last_n
args.help: Keep only the N newest matched events.

cli.usage: sandbox-observability-cli events --sandbox-id ID [--name NAME] [--since-ms MS] [--last-n N]

operations.name: cgroup
operations.summary: Resource series for a scope (cpu/mem/io + disk).
operations.description: Fold the sample log for one scope into a time series with deltas: cgroup counters (cpu/mem/io from /sys/fs/cgroup) plus the disk sample (upperdir bytes/files) carried in the same record.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

args.name: scope
args.help: Resource scope: 'sandbox' or a workspace id.

args.name: window_ms
args.help: Lookback window in milliseconds (max 600000).

operations.name: layerstack
operations.summary: Per-layer leasing/booking inventory, and stack series.
operations.description: Show the active manifest as a per-layer inventory: disk bytes, how many workspaces lease each layer, and which leased layers book each base. Served live from the runtime; does not read the log.

args.name: sandbox_id
args.help: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

args.name: workspace_id
args.help: Show one workspace's lower layers and private upperdir.

args.name: window_ms
args.help: Lookback window in milliseconds for the stack trend (max 600000).
